title: capability negotiation
manager: Capability negotiation — a machine declares which host capabilities it needs, and a host declares which it provides, so mismatches are caught before running rather than crashing mid-execution. This is what keeps the fleet honest: a machine that needs durable storage or a real clock won't silently run degraded on a host that lacks them; the negotiation surfaces the gap as a clear, up-front error the moment you try to deploy.
cs: target capability declaration + negotiation (fsl#1172/#1173, §16 specced); host-provides / machine-requires, matched before run; mismatch is an up-front error.
